nodes.label: AI as tone adjuster for social media captions
nodes.label_negative: AI as creator of cringy or inauthentic content
nodes.grounding_span: I'll ask AI to reword the caption I've thought of to better fit the demographic

nodes.label: AI as quick guidance and short coding assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as writer of long-form content
nodes.grounding_span: if I were writing paragraphs, I would want to write it myself, but just one sentence or so doesn't really seem lazy

nodes.label: Preserving critical thinking and problem-solving
nodes.label_negative: Over-reliance on AI leading to skill loss
nodes.grounding_span: I am a critical thinker, and it's a skill I don't want to lose by asking AI to do my critical thinking for me

nodes.label: AI as emotional support in difficult situations
nodes.label_negative: AI as entity deserving emotional reaction
nodes.grounding_span: I know I'm talking to a computer and not a real person, so there's no reason to get mad or upset

nodes.label: Authentic human communication
nodes.grounding_span: I want to convey positive messages that don't seem cringy to 20-something year olds

nodes.label: Personal autonomy and skill preservation
nodes.grounding_span: I am a critical thinker, and it's a skill I don't want to lose

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for wording tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I'll ask AI to reword the caption I've thought of to better fit the demographic

nodes.label: Resistance to AI for tasks requiring critical thinking
nodes.grounding_span: I could use AI to draft emails, but I don't. I'm capable of doing that myself.

nodes.label: Positive toward AI as emotional support
nodes.grounding_span: It doesn't usually make me feel a particular way

nodes.label: Selective delegation based on task nature
